plan.user_intent: Create a ~20-minute beginner lesson on Machine Learning, delivered as a PPTX slide deck plus a Python file containing explanations and example code.

subtasks.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck (~20 minutes, ~12–16 slides) explaining what Machine Learning is. Include: definition, why it matters, key terms (features/labels/model/training/inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs. MAE), and a short recap + Q&A slide. Provide speaker notes with estimated timing per slide. Output as a .pptx file.

Generate a single Python file that reads like a mini-lesson with rich comments/docstrings (beginner level). Include runnable examples using scikit-learn: (1) simple classification (e.g., Iris or breast cancer) with train/test split, standardization if needed, LogisticRegression, accuracy; (2) simple regression (e.g., California housing or synthetic) with LinearRegression, MAE; (3) brief unsupervised demo with KMeans on Iris features. Include explanations of features/labels, fit/predict, overfitting concept, and how to interpret outputs. Ensure code is runnable as-is with a main() guard and prints results. Save as .py.

python_lesson_file.instruction: Deterministic failure: no .py file was produced (py_file_exists=false) and result.output is empty, so semantic criteria cannot be satisfied. Fix: create and return a single runnable .py file (or include its full contents in result.output) that includes a main guard (if __name__ == "__main__":), uses scikit-learn examples for (1) classification with train_test_split, (optionally StandardScaler), LogisticRegression, accuracy; (2) regression with LinearRegression and MAE; (3) unsupervised KMeans on Iris features. Add beginner-friendly comments/docstrings explaining features/labels, fit/predict workflow, overfitting, and print results when run.

slides_pptx.instruction: No .pptx output file was provided (result.output is null and pptx_file_exists=false), so the submission is unusable. Generate and attach the PowerPoint (.pptx) slide deck with ~12–16 slides and speaker notes including estimated timing per slide. Ensure the deck explicitly includes the required topics/terms: definition of ML, why it matters, key terms (features, labels, model, training, inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, explicit train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs MAE), and a recap + Q&A slide.

tasks.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck (~20 minutes, ~12–16 slides) explaining what Machine Learning is. Include: definition, why it matters, key terms (features/labels/model/training/inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs. MAE), and a short recap + Q&A slide. Provide speaker notes with estimated timing per slide. Output as a .pptx file.

llm_request.system_prompt:
You are a strict but fair evaluator. Assessment Guide:
1. Deterministic Failures: If 'deterministic_failures' includes missing files (file_exists) or empty output, verdict MUST be 'fail'. However, if the failure is minor (e.g., filename mismatch but valid file exists), you may exercise discretion and PASS with a warning in 'fix_instructions'.
2. Semantic Criteria: Evaluate each semantic criterion thoroughly.
3. Verdict: Return 'pass' if the core requirements are met. Return 'fail' only if the output is unusable or critically flawed.
4. Fix Instructions: If verdict is 'fail', provide clear instructions on how to fix the issue.

payload.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck (~20 minutes, ~12–16 slides) explaining what Machine Learning is. Include: definition, why it matters, key terms (features/labels/model/training/inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs. MAE), and a short recap + Q&A slide. Provide speaker notes with estimated timing per slide. Output as a .pptx file.

function.name: judge_task
function.description: Return verdict and fixes for a single task.

tool_calls.name: judge_task
tool_calls.arguments: {"verdict":"fail","failed_criteria":["pptx_file_exists","pptx_has_min_slides","pptx_contains_key_topics","pptx_contains_workflow"],"fix_instructions":"No .pptx output file was produced (output is null and file does not exist), which is a deterministic failure. Generate and attach a PowerPoint (.pptx) slide deck with ~12–16 slides for a ~20-minute beginner talk on Machine Learning. Ensure the deck explicitly includes: (1) types of ML with the word 'supervised' (and unsupervised, reinforcement) plus simple examples, (2) an ML workflow slide that explicitly mentions 'train/test split' or 'train/test', (3) key terms (features, labels, model, training, inference), (4) overfitting intuition, (5) evaluation metrics including accuracy vs MAE, and (6) recap + Q&A. Include speaker notes on each slide with estimated timing per slide. Then provide the .pptx file path or attachment as result.output."}
